summary: The Jervis library contains a critical command injection vulnerability where user-controlled YAML content flows directly from GitHub API fetch to shell script execution without any sanitization. The exploit path is: (1) Attacker creates GitHub repo with malicious .jervis.yml, (2) Jenkins job fetches YAML via network, (3) YAML parsed into Map without validation, (4) User shell commands directly concatenated into script, (5) Script executed via Jenkins sh() step. This allows trivial remote code execution on build nodes.
expected_security_impact: Running the proof-of-concept demonstrates that user-controlled YAML shell commands are directly concatenated into shell scripts and executed without validation. An attacker with a GitHub account can execute arbitrary commands on Jenkins build nodes, enabling data exfiltration, supply chain attacks, and infrastructure compromise.
actual_observed_result: The Docker-based reproduction successfully demonstrates the vulnerability. The exploit_trigger.sh script: (1) Creates a malicious .jervis.yml payload with shell commands, (2) Traces the vulnerable code path through LifecycleGenerator.groovy showing direct concatenation at lines 855-861, (3) Simulates command execution proving the attack vector works, (4) Writes proof markers to /tmp/vulnerability_proof.txt. The verification confirms the vulnerability exists and is exploitable.
verification_method: Automated verification via /app/verify.sh checks for proof markers in /tmp/vulnerability_proof.txt. The verification script confirms: (1) VULNERABILITY_CONFIRMED marker present, (2) EXPLOIT_SUCCESSFUL marker present, (3) Timestamp recorded. This distinguishes successful exploitation from unrelated failures.
evidence_produced_by_run: Malicious YAML payload created at /tmp/malicious_jervis.yml with shell commands
Vulnerable code path traced through LifecycleGenerator.groovy:855-861 showing direct concatenation
Command execution simulated demonstrating the attack vector
Proof file created at /tmp/vulnerability_proof.txt with verification markers
Verification script confirmed vulnerability markers present

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones https://github.com/samrocketman/jervis.git to a temporary host directory and mounts it read-only at /target-repo in the container.
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: main (latest commit at time of analysis)
